evidence.scientific_clarity: 6-cluster ridgeline KDE plot directly fulfills the brief; cluster ordering and distribution shifts are clearly visible with left-side labels.
evidence.typography: Script sets no pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype=0; pdffonts confirms Type 3 (BMQQDV+DejaVuSans) — the matplotlib default non-editable bitmap font.
evidence.palette: Sequential blue-to-green palette is appropriate for ordered clusters and avoids red/green directional misuse, but blue-green transitions are problematic for deuteranopia.
evidence.layout_density: overlap=0.55 gives mild ridge overlap as briefed; left-side labels are clear; x-axis padding is reasonable with no obvious wasted space.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption includes specific numerical claims and matches the figure; however, non-selectable Type 3 fonts and DejaVu Sans default would draw reviewer criticism.
evidence.vector_fidelity: pdffonts reports Type 3 encoding for DejaVuSans — text is not selectable or editable; script never sets matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype'] = 42.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (39 KB), figure.png (72 KB), caption.tex (463 B), gen_figure.py (2.3 KB), and bonus figure.svg all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names the plot type, states bandwidth=0.3, and gives specific median values (Cluster 1 ≈1.9, Cluster 6 ≈3.4) as a concrete numerical finding.

overall_strengths: All required output files are present and non-trivial, including a bonus SVG; the figure faithfully executes the 6-cluster ridgeline brief with clear left-side cluster labels.
Caption is publication-quality: it names the method, states the bandwidth parameter, and anchors the finding with specific numerical values for the extreme clusters.
overall_weaknesses: No pdf.fonttype=42 (or svg.fonttype=0) set anywhere in the script — pdffonts confirms Type 3 DejaVu Sans, meaning text is bitmap-encoded and not selectable/editable in the PDF.

Default DejaVu Sans font is used throughout; a publication-ready figure should use a serif or clean sans-serif (e.g. Helvetica/Arial via rcParams) and the palette has colorblind accessibility gaps for deuteranopes.
